name: Concluding remarks on G-stability
statement_text: A G-stable linear multistep method is A-stable.
proof_text:

half-plane. Form the inner product W โ M W , where M is the matrix given by

so that Re ฮฑ(w)/ฮฒ(w) > 0.
proof_latex:
\begin{proof}
We use the criterion that if $|w| < 1$, then $z = \alpha(w)/\beta(w)$ is in the right half-plane. Form the inner product $W^* M W$, where $M$ is the matrix given by \eqref{eq:451e} and
\[
W = \begin{bmatrix}
1 \\
w \\
w^2 \\
\vdots \\
w^k
\end{bmatrix}.
\]
We find that
\[
\alpha(w)\overline{\beta(w)} + \overline{\alpha(w)}\beta(w) = W^* M W + (1 - |w|^2) \sum_{j,l=1}^k g_{jl} w^{j-1} \overline{w}^{l-1} > 0,
\]
so that $\operatorname{Re}\bigl( \alpha(w)/\beta(w) \bigr) > 0$.
\end{proof}
context_latex:
\paragraph{Context}
The theorem concerns linear multistep methods and their stability properties. The preceding text introduces notation for analyzing order and local error. The theorem statement is self-contained, referring only to the standard definitions of G-stability and A-stability for numerical methods for ODEs.
preamble: The theorem is about linear multistep methods and their stability properties. The preceding text introduces notation for analyzing order and local error, but the theorem statement itself is self-contained, referring only to the definitions of G-stability and A-stability, which are standard concepts in the context of numerical methods for ODEs.
